title: Develop adrs CLI skill for Architecture Decision Records
description:
## Goal

Develop a Claude Code skill for using the `adrs` CLI tool to manage Architecture Decision Records (ADRs).

## Scope

### CLI User Guide

- Installation and setup
- Core commands: `adrs init`, `adrs new`, `adrs list`, `adrs link`, `adrs generate`
- Configuration options and defaults
- Directory structure conventions

### Common Patterns

- When to create an ADR (decision triggers)
- Naming conventions (NNNN-title-with-dashes.md)
- Status lifecycle: proposed → accepted → deprecated → superseded
- Linking related ADRs (supersedes, amends, links)
- Tagging and categorization strategies
- Review and approval workflows

### Anti-Patterns

- ADRs that are too granular (implementation details)
- ADRs that are too broad (multiple decisions in one)
- Missing context or consequences
- Not updating status when decisions change
- Orphaned ADRs (no links to related decisions)
- Writing ADRs after the fact without noting it

### Templates

- Standard ADR template (title, status, context, decision, consequences)
- Lightweight ADR (for smaller decisions)
- Technical spike ADR (for research outcomes)
- Deprecation ADR (for sunsetting decisions)
- Security decision ADR (with threat model section)

### Integration

- Git hooks for ADR validation
- CI checks for ADR completeness
- Linking ADRs to issues/PRs
- Generating ADR index/table of contents

## Deliverables

- [ ] `context/skills/adrs/SKILL.md` - Main skill document
- [ ] `context/skills/adrs/templates/` - ADR templates
- [ ] `context/skills/adrs/examples/` - Example ADRs

## References

- adrs CLI: https://github.com/joshka/adrs (or similar tool)
- ADR GitHub org: https://adr.github.io/
- Michael Nygard's original ADR blog post

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Skill covers full adrs CLI usage
- [ ] Includes at least 3 reusable templates
- [ ] Documents common patterns with examples
- [ ] Lists anti-patterns with explanations
- [ ] Tested with real project ADR workflow

title: Research skill discovery: registries, GitHub patterns, and crawling strategies
description:
## Goal

Research and document all methods for discovering Claude Code skills from community sources.

## Plan

See `.claude/plans/skill-discovery/research-plan.md` for full details.

## Phases

### Phase 1: Registry Discovery
- Verify ccpm, claude-plugins.dev, npm, PyPI, crates.io
- Document APIs and search endpoints
- Deliverable: `registries.yaml`

### Phase 2: GitHub Search Patterns
- File-based searches (SKILL.md, plugin.json)
- Content-based searches (frontmatter, structure)
- Repository and topic searches
- Deliverable: `github-queries.md`

### Phase 3: Web Crawling Strategies
- Target sites: Dev.to, Reddit, HN, Medium
- Crawling tools: crawl4ai, firecrawl, WebFetch
- Platform-specific queries
- Deliverable: `crawl-targets.yaml`

### Phase 4: Aggregation & Indexing
- Data model for skills
- SQLite + FTS5 storage
- Deliverable: `skill-index.db`

### Phase 5: Integration
- Update plugin-skill-researcher agent
- CLI tools via justfile
- Documentation

### Phase 6: Validation
- Test queries against known skills
- Quality metrics (recall, precision, freshness)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 3+ skill registries documented with APIs
- [ ] GitHub search finds 50+ community skills
- [ ] Web crawling discovers 20+ additional skills
- [ ] Unified index searchable in <100ms
- [ ] Integration with existing agents complete

## References

- Plan: `.claude/plans/skill-discovery/research-plan.md`
- Related: `plugin-skill-researcher` agent

title: Develop plugin-version-sync hook for plugin.json → marketplace.json
description:
## Goal

Develop a Claude Code hook that automatically syncs plugin versions between `plugin.json` and `marketplace.json` when a plugin's version is updated.

## Requirements

### Core Functionality

1. **Version change detection**: Use JSON-aware tooling to detect if a change to `plugin.json` was a version update (not just any edit)
   - Options: AST-grep, difftastic, jq diff, or custom tool
   - Must distinguish version bumps from other edits

2. **Sync to marketplace.json**: When version change detected:
   - Find matching plugin entry in `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` by name
   - Update the `version` field to match plugin.json
   - Preserve all other fields

3. **New plugin detection**: Detect if a plugin.json exists but isn't tracked in marketplace.json
   - Configurable behavior: auto-add or warn-only
   - If auto-add enabled, create new entry with required fields

### Implementation Options

**Option A: Shell pipe (preferred if simple enough)**
```bash
# Pseudocode
jq -r '.version' "$PLUGIN_JSON" | xargs -I{} jq --arg v {} \
  '.plugins |= map(if .name == "PLUGIN_NAME" then .version = $v else . end)' \
  marketplace.json
```

**Option B: Custom CLI tool**
- Language: Python, Rust, or Go
- Single binary, minimal dependencies
- Commands: `plugin-sync check`, `plugin-sync update`, `plugin-sync add`

### Hook Integration

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PostToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Write|Edit",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "bash -c 'case \"$TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH\" in */plugin.json) plugin-version-sync \"$TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH\";; esac'",
            "timeout": 10
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Configuration

Support config via environment or config file:
- `PLUGIN_SYNC_AUTO_ADD=true|false` - Auto-add new plugins to marketplace
- `PLUGIN_SYNC_MARKETPLACE_PATH` - Path to marketplace.json (default: `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detects version changes in plugin.json (not other edits)
- [ ] Updates corresponding entry in marketplace.json
- [ ] Handles missing plugin gracefully (configurable: add or warn)
- [ ] Works as Claude Code hook (PostToolUse)
- [ ] Fast execution (<1s)
- [ ] Tested with design-to-code plugin workflow

## Technical Notes

- marketplace.json structure: `{ "plugins": [{ "name": "...", "version": "...", ... }] }`
- plugin.json location: `context/plugins/<name>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`
- Hook should be idempotent (safe to run multiple times)

## Related

- `.claude/rules/plugin-version-sync.md` - Manual sync rule (to be replaced by this hook)

title: Develop git-cliff CLI skill for changelog generation
description:
## Goal

Develop a Claude Code skill for using the `git-cliff` CLI tool to generate changelogs from git history using conventional commits.

## Scope

### CLI User Guide

- Installation methods (cargo, brew, npm, binary)
- Core commands: `git-cliff`, `git-cliff --init`, `git-cliff --latest`, `git-cliff --unreleased`
- Configuration: `cliff.toml` setup and options
- Output formats: markdown, JSON, custom templates
- Version tagging integration
- Remote integration (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, Bitbucket)

### Configuration Deep Dive

- `cliff.toml` anatomy
- Commit parsers and filters
- Commit preprocessors (regex transformations)
- Changelog sections (features, fixes, breaking changes, etc.)
- Conventional commit scope mapping
- Link generation (commits, PRs, issues, authors)
- Remote API integration for PR/issue metadata

### Common Patterns

- Conventional commit format enforcement
- Semantic versioning alignment
- Monorepo changelog strategies (per-package vs unified)
- Release workflow integration
- GitHub Release body generation
- Keep-a-Changelog format compliance
- Automating changelog in CI/CD
- Generating changelogs for version ranges

### Anti-Patterns

- Non-conventional commit messages breaking parsing
- Overly complex commit parser regex
- Not filtering out noise commits (chore, ci, etc.)
- Generating changelog without version tags
- Manual changelog edits that get overwritten
- Ignoring breaking change footers

### Templates

- Standard changelog (Keep-a-Changelog style)
- GitHub Releases template
- Minimal changelog (just features and fixes)
- Detailed changelog (with authors and links)
- Scoped changelog (for monorepos)
- Security-focused changelog

### Integration

- GitHub Actions workflow for auto-changelog
- Pre-release hook for changelog generation
- Semantic-release integration
- Release-please compatibility
- Commitlint + git-cliff workflow

## Deliverables

- [ ] `context/skills/git-cliff/SKILL.md` - Main skill document
- [ ] `context/skills/git-cliff/templates/` - cliff.toml templates
- [ ] `context/skills/git-cliff/examples/` - Example configurations
- [ ] `context/skills/git-cliff/workflows/` - CI/CD examples

## References

- Homepage: https://git-cliff.org/
- Repository: https://github.com/orhun/git-cliff
- Documentation: https://git-cliff.org/docs/
- Configuration reference: https://git-cliff.org/docs/configuration
- Template syntax: https://git-cliff.org/docs/templating

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Skill covers full git-cliff CLI usage
- [ ] Includes cliff.toml templates for common use cases
- [ ] Documents configuration options with examples
- [ ] Covers remote integration (GitHub PR/issue links)
- [ ] Includes CI/CD workflow examples
- [ ] Lists anti-patterns with explanations
- [ ] Tested with real project changelog generation

title: Develop git-bug CLI skill for distributed bug tracking
description:
## Goal

Develop a Claude Code skill for using the `git-bug` CLI tool for distributed, offline-first bug tracking embedded in git.

## Pre-Development: Research Alternatives

Before developing the skill, research and compare alternatives to ensure git-bug is the best choice:

### Candidates to Evaluate

| Tool | Repository | Focus |
|------|------------|-------|
| git-bug | https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug | Distributed bug tracking in git |
| youtrack-cli | https://github.com/shanehofstetter/youtrack-cli | JetBrains YouTrack CLI |
| BugForge | https://github.com/Mogbur/BugForge | Bug tracking tool |
| git-issue | https://github.com/dspinellis/git-issue | Distributed issue management |
| ticket | https://github.com/mikeslattery/ticket | Git-based issue tracking |

### Evaluation Criteria

- [ ] Active maintenance (commits in last 6 months)
- [ ] Documentation quality
- [ ] Feature completeness (create, list, edit, close, labels, assignees)
- [ ] Offline-first capability
- [ ] Git integration depth
- [ ] Export/import capabilities
- [ ] Bridge to external trackers (GitHub, GitLab, Jira)
- [ ] Community adoption (stars, forks, issues)

### Research Deliverable

Create `.plans/skills/git-bug/research.md` with:
- Comparison matrix of all candidates
- Recommendation with rationale
- If git-bug confirmed, proceed with skill development
- If alternative preferred, adjust skill scope accordingly

---

## Skill Development (post-research)

### CLI User Guide

- Installation methods (go install, brew, binary)
- Core commands: `git bug add`, `git bug ls`, `git bug show`, `git bug label`, `git bug status`
- Interactive TUI: `git bug termui`
- Web UI: `git bug webui`
- Bridge commands: `git bug bridge`

### Common Patterns

- Creating bugs with metadata (title, message, labels)
- Querying and filtering bugs
- Commenting on bugs
- Status workflow: open → closed
- Label taxonomy strategies
- Bridging to GitHub/GitLab issues
- Team workflows with distributed sync

### Anti-Patterns

- Using for non-bug work items (features, epics)
- Ignoring bridge sync conflicts
- Overly complex label hierarchies
- Not pushing bug refs regularly
- Mixing git-bug with external tracker as source of truth

### Templates

- Bug report template
- Feature request template (if applicable)
- Security issue template
- Quick bug (minimal fields)

### Integration

- Git aliases for common operations
- Pre-commit hooks for bug references
- CI integration for bug status checks
- Bridge configuration for GitHub/GitLab

## Deliverables

- [ ] `.plans/skills/git-bug/research.md` - Alternative comparison
- [ ] `context/skills/git-bug/SKILL.md` - Main skill document
- [ ] `context/skills/git-bug/templates/` - Bug templates
- [ ] `context/skills/git-bug/bridges/` - Bridge configurations

## References

- git-bug: https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug
- git-bug documentation: https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug/blob/master/doc/

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Research phase completed with documented recommendation
- [ ] Skill covers full CLI usage
- [ ] Includes bridge configuration examples
- [ ] Documents common patterns with examples
- [ ] Lists anti-patterns with explanations
- [ ] Tested with real project workflow
